---
title: "MV3_Mandarin_exploratory_analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2025-07-27"
---
```{r}
# load necessary libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(reshape2)
```


```{r}
# Elicit information relevant to musicianship
music_info <- read_csv(file="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/comp-music-lab/manyvoices3/refs/heads/main/JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Exploratory%20analysis/Mandarin_song_speech_0520.csv") %>%
  dplyr::select(`renumbered ID`, musicianship_self_perceived, musicianship_compliment)

effectsize_f0 <- read_csv(file='https://raw.githubusercontent.com/comp-music-lab/manyvoices3/refs/heads/main/JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Confirmatory%20analysis/data/pitch%20processed%20zero/f0_cohend_results.csv') %>%
  mutate(Feature = "Pitch Height")
merged_f0 <- effectsize_f0 %>%
  left_join(music_info, by = c("SpeakerID" = "renumbered ID"))
print(merged_f0)

effectsize_f0stab <- read_csv(file='https://raw.githubusercontent.com/comp-music-lab/manyvoices3/refs/heads/main/JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Confirmatory%20analysis/data/pitch%20processed%20zero/f0stab_cohend_results.csv') %>%
  mutate(Feature = "Pitch Stability")
merged_f0stab <- effectsize_f0stab %>%
  left_join(music_info, by = c("SpeakerID" = "renumbered ID"))

print(merged_f0stab)
effectsize_IOI <- read_csv(file='https://raw.githubusercontent.com/comp-music-lab/manyvoices3/refs/heads/main/JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Confirmatory%20analysis/data/IOI/IOI_cohend_results.csv') %>%
  mutate(Feature = "IOI Rate")
merged_IOI <- effectsize_IOI %>%
  left_join(music_info, by = c("SpeakerID" = "renumbered ID"))
print(merged_IOI)
```
```{r}
# plot musicianship (self_perceived) with cohen'd
merged_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  merged_f0,
  merged_f0stab,
  merged_IOI
)
merged_all <- merged_all %>%
  mutate(Feature = ifelse(Feature == "IOI Rate", "Temporal Rate", Feature))

cor_stats <- merged_all %>%
  group_by(Feature) %>%
  summarise(
    r = cor(Cohens_d, musicianship_self_perceived, use = "complete.obs"),
    p = cor.test(Cohens_d, musicianship_self_perceived)$p.value
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0("r = ", round(r, 2))
  )

label_positions <- tibble(
  Feature = c("Pitch Height", "Pitch Stability", "Temporal Rate"),
  x = c(Inf, Inf, Inf),
  y = c(100, 94, 88),
  hjust = 1.1
)

cor_labels <- left_join(cor_stats, label_positions, by = "Feature")

p1 <- ggplot(merged_all, aes(x = Cohens_d, y = musicianship_self_perceived, color = Feature)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.2, fill = "gray70") +
  geom_text(
    data = cor_labels,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = Feature, hjust = hjust),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    show.legend =FALSE,
    size = 5
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Cohen's d",
    y = "Musicianship (self-perceived)",
    color = "Acoustic features"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

show(p1)

cor_stats %>%
  dplyr::select(Feature, r, p) %>%
  mutate(
    r = round(r, 2),
    p = ifelse(p < .001, "< .001", round(p, 3))
  )
ggsave("./JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Exploratory analysis/figures/musicianship_self_perceived.png", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```


```{r}
# plot musicianship (compliment) with cohen'd
merged_all <- merged_all %>%
  mutate(Feature = ifelse(Feature == "IOI Rate", "Temporal Rate", Feature))

cor_stats <- merged_all %>%
  group_by(Feature) %>%
  summarise(
    r = round(cor(Cohens_d, musicianship_compliment, use = "complete.obs"), 2),  
    p = cor.test(Cohens_d, musicianship_compliment)$p.value
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0("r = ", r)
  )

label_positions <- tibble(
  Feature = c("Pitch Height", "Pitch Stability", "Temporal Rate"),
  x = c(Inf, Inf, Inf),
  y = c(100, 94, 88),
  hjust = 1.1
)

cor_labels <- left_join(cor_stats, label_positions, by = "Feature")

p2 <- ggplot(merged_all, aes(x = Cohens_d, y = musicianship_compliment, color = Feature)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, size = 1.2, fill = "grey70") +
  geom_text(
    data = cor_labels,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = Feature, hjust = hjust),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    show.legend =FALSE,
    size = 5
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Cohen's d",
    y = "Musicianship (complimented)",
    color = "Type"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

cor_stats %>%
  dplyr::select(Feature, r, p) %>%
  mutate(
    r = round(r, 2),
    p = ifelse(p < .001, "< .001", round(p, 3))
  )
show(p2)

p1_no_legend <- p1 + theme(legend.position = "none")
combined_plot <- p1_no_legend | p2 

combined_plot

ggsave("./JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Exploratory analysis/figures/musicianship_cohend_plots.png", combined_plot, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#plot bar chart showing the distribution of musicianship-related variables
data <- read_csv(file="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/comp-music-lab/manyvoices3/refs/heads/main/JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Exploratory%20analysis/Mandarin_song_speech_0520.csv")
cols <- c("know_song",
          "musicianship_self_perceived",
          "musicianship_compliment",
          "like_song",
          "sing_regularly")

df_long <- melt(data[, cols], variable.name = "Measure", value.name = "Score")

summary_df <- aggregate(Score ~ Measure, data = df_long, 
                        FUN = function(x) c(mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                            se = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(sum(!is.na(x)))))
summary_df <- do.call(data.frame, summary_df)
colnames(summary_df) <- c("Measure", "Mean", "SE")

summary_df$Measure <- factor(summary_df$Measure, 
                             levels = c("know_song",
                                        "musicianship_self_perceived",
                                        "musicianship_compliment",
                                        "like_song",
                                        "sing_regularly"),
                             labels = c("Song Familiarity",
                                        "Musicianship\n (self-perceived)",
                                        "Musicianship\n (complimented)",
                                        "Song Liking",
                                        "Singing Regularity"))

df_long$Measure <- factor(df_long$Measure, 
                          levels = c("know_song",
                                     "musicianship_self_perceived",
                                     "musicianship_compliment",
                                     "like_song",
                                     "sing_regularly"),
                          labels = c("Song Familiarity",
                                     "Musicianship\n (self-perceived)",
                                     "Musicianship\n (complimented)",
                                     "Song Liking",
                                     "Singing Regularity"))

p <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = Measure, y = Mean, fill = Measure)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7, color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = df_long, 
             aes(x = Measure, y = Score, color = Measure),
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0),
             size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Mean - SE, ymax = Mean + SE), width = 0.2) +
  labs(y = "Score", x = NULL, size = 12) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 105),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 25),
    expand = c(0, 0.5)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, color = "black", size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black"),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1")

show(p)

ggsave("./JiaEtAl(MandarinAuckland)/Exploratory analysis/figures/musicianship_summary_barplot.png",
       plot = p, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in", dpi = 300)

```
```{r}

```

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
